item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops! Ignore the first version of this comment. It was meant for someone else. Anyhow, this is a very interesting skip. I feel like a little bit of it is flying over my head, but I like the interconnectedness of it. The TV is a weird anomaly, the interaction with another anomaly is strange (I bet I'd get it more if I read the related skip), I like the "small thing creates huge implications when anomalies combine" because that's what makes most sense to me. Like, if these things work off of nonsense-logic, of course two things that seem completely safe have the potential to fuck the whole world up if you put them next to each other. That's why the Foundation is so anal about containment!

+1, if you couldn't tell, and more good Fifthist content is always appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just cannot find the words to express how much I love this article, but hell I'm going to try. Everything about it is so wonderful, from slowly revealing piece by piece what happened to having SCP-3125 die through accidental exposure to bees. This is honestly the best thing I could have ever gotten from the contest. Thank you so much Ais :)

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: My gift for Art Exchange 2020! I made a little piece for the wonderful Deadly Bread featuring one of my favorite skips from them, SCP-5993! Hope you also enjoy it.
